$meta.name: macOS Tahoe calibration tokens
$meta.purpose: Single source of truth for pixel-parity work: theme scripts + Liquid Glass shader read from here.
$meta.principle: When a value is ambiguous, Apple HIG + Tahoe screenshots are ground truth. These are our own DERIVED measurements/specs, not reproductions of Apple assets.
$meta.confidence: high = Apple-documented or trivially measured; med = consistent across teardowns; low = reasoned estimate, measure directly before shipping
$meta.status: reconciled against corpus audit. Items still marked low need a direct screenshot measurement.
$meta.sources: Apple HIG (Materials, Motion), Apple newsroom (Liquid Glass 2025), SwiftUI docs (ConcentricRectangle, Material), WWDC25 AppKit design, lapcatsoftware teardowns, MacRumors threads, macos-defaults.com, SF Pro optical-size docs
$meta.units: pt = logical points (≈2px on Retina); px = physical pixels; u = shader uniform (0..~2)

window.cornerRadius.compactToolbar.note: Apple reference 'Window corners': Compact toolbar window = 20pt

window.cornerRadius.toolbar.note: Apple reference 'Window corners': Toolbar window = 26pt (my @2x measurement of ~24 validated within 2pt). Discrete tiers, not continuous

squircle.exponent.note: best superellipse fit n≈4 (≈Figma smoothing 0.6); Apple's real corner is tuned Bezier, not a pure superellipse

concentricity.rule.note: shared curve center; corners far from an edge default to radius 0

trafficLight.spacing.note: MEASURED center-to-center on a Finder capture @2x (46-47px/1.98)

trafficLight.inset.note: MEASURED consensus across 27 windows: control-center inset from window edge @2x (median 26.1pt)

vibrancy.levels.note: foreground contrast tiers; quaternary not for thin materials

material.states.note: Apple-documented Liquid Glass appearances: Clear = more transparent; Tinted = more opacity + contrast (user-selectable)

adaptiveInk.behavior.note: the 'dynamic ink' — text/symbols adapt to backdrop to stay legible; compositor-only CANNOT do it, needs toolkit cooperation on Linux

liquidGlass.mechanism.note: refraction, NOT scatter-blur — validates the shader's refraction pass

glass.refraction.note: lensing magnitude; motion-dependent, tune in the Lab

glass.chromatic.note: edge realism; not a named Apple feature — keep barely-there

blur.sigma.note: our frost approximation; the real mechanism is lensing

dock.magnifiedSize.note: GUI slider max; Terminal largesize to 512. No true parabolic zoom on Linux

spacing.grid.note: Apple convention (4pt half-steps), not a Tahoe-published spec

control.buttonHeight.note: classic regular push-button; Settings uses list rows, so no bare button was in the measured shots

control.toolbarHeight.note: Tahoe glass toolbars are taller; unpublished

card.cornerRadius.note: MEASURED: grouped list card @2x; inner-container radius, distinct from window radius

list.rowHeight.note: MEASURED: Settings list-row pitch @2x (~86px/1.9); some variance from group gaps

weight.appName.note: app name in menu bar; Apple publishes no per-element weight table

baseline.duration.note: AppKit NSAnimationContext default — the one hard duration constant Apple documents

spring.presetDuration.note: SwiftUI .smooth/.snappy/.bouncy all 0.5s; differ only by bounce 0 / 0.15 / 0.3 (= 1 - dampingFraction)

spring.smooth.note: SwiftUI .smooth (response 0.5, damping 1.0) — critically damped, no overshoot

spring.interactive.note: SwiftUI .interactiveSpring (response 0.15, damping 0.86) — gesture-velocity handoff via initial-velocity

spring.mapping.note: EXACT SwiftUI->libadwaita conversion — same damped-oscillator ODE, not an approximation

motion.feasibility.note: NOT themeable (no CSS spring property); GNOME Shell + KWin do easing curves (Back/Elastic fake overshoot), not real physics

liquidGlass.motion.note: behavior only — Apple publishes NO numeric motion constants for Liquid Glass

accent.default.note: control-TINT blue (accepted sRGB); Tahoe swatch unpublished. Distinct from the deeper sidebar SELECTION fill below

selection.fill.note: MEASURED: sidebar selected-row fill @2x; deeper than the control tint — macOS uses different tint vs selection blues

accent.palette.value: Blue #4380E8, Purple #93359D, Pink #CF6195, Red #C2413C, Orange #D97A39, Yellow #DBB047, Green #66A845, Graphite #85A6C8
accent.palette.note: MEASURED by differencing the 8 accent samples (median of accent-colored pixels)

accent.recolors.value: buttons, pop-ups, radios/checks, focus rings, selection
